page: Microsoft Azure D64nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine instance belonging to the Dnlv6 family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled, though the instance is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities across compute, memory, and network-bound tasks. The instance achieves top-tier performance in static web serving throughput, reaching 89.28 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached and uncached operations place in the top 25%, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 4126.81 GB/sec. Cryptographic and integer operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by strong Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction scores. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations, which reached 72.31 million operations per second, also rank in the top 25%, while stress-ng CPU performance remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64nls_v6 offers a specialized hardware profile that minimizes memory overhead to maximize compute density. By allocating 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, it avoids the expense of unused memory for workloads that are primarily CPU-bound. This makes the instance highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, network-optimized appliances, multi-threaded compression tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its Intel AMX instructions can be leveraged. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or applications requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D64nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine from the Dnlv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 physical cores clocked at 4.2 GHz, supported by 128.0 GB of RAM and a 260 MB L3 cache. The architecture includes Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance relies on premium remote storage. Benchmarks show top-tier static web serving throughput of 89.28 GB/sec and strong memory bandwidth. It is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, network-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints, such as high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier web serving throughput, ideal for compute-heavy and network-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64nls_v6 is a network-optimized, 64-vCPU Intel Xeon virtual machine with 128 GB RAM, built for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A high-compute, network-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and low memory density for efficient CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a lean 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier static web serving throughput of 89.28 GB/sec
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Optimized for high-throughput network appliances and CPU-bound inference
Premium storage capable with no local disk overhead
